rules.title: Trendlines Are Drawn From Wicks
rules.text: Downtrend lines connect swing high wicks. Uptrend lines connect swing low wicks. The agent auto-detects these from the 2 most recent swing pivots using a 2-bar lookback on each side.

rules.title: Long = Body Close Above Downtrend Line
rules.text: The candle BODY (not wick) must close above the projected downtrend line. A wick above and close back below is a fake-out — body close is the confirmation.

rules.title: Short = Body Close Below Uptrend Line
rules.text: The candle BODY must close below the projected uptrend line. Same logic inverted — body close is the only valid confirmation.

rules.title: Accumulation = Compression Before the Break
rules.text: 8–15 bars of tight, ranging candles near the trendline before the breakout. This is the 'solid' base. No accumulation = lower confidence, noted in the log but not blocked.

rules.title: Minimum 1:3 R:R
rules.text: Stop goes below the breakout candle low (long) or above the breakout candle high (short). Target must be at least 3× the risk distance — next swing high/low or clear level.

rules.title: EMA = Confluence Only
rules.text: EMA direction adds confidence but doesn't trigger. Conflicting EMA (e.g. bearish EMA on a long) is noted but doesn't block the trade — trendline break is the primary signal.

rules.title: EMA Breakout = Strong Body at EMA Support
rules.text: Price is inside or touching the EMA band. A strong bull body candle forms at MA Top or MA Bottom support — body must be 60%+ of the candle range. Wick above is acceptable (momentum), wick through the EMA below and close back above is the ideal entry. Target is next unswept liquidity level with minimum 3:1 R:R.
